findings.title: Installed on-the-fly via a package runner
findings.detail: Server 'mcp-searxng' is fetched and executed at launch via 'npx' with auto-accept flag(s) -y; package spec: mcp-searxng.
findings.recommendation: Install the server as a pinned dependency and invoke the local binary, or vendor it. If a runner is unavoidable, pin an exact version (see unpinned_source).

findings.title: Package source is unpinned or mutable

findings.recommendation: Pin an exact version (pkg@1.2.3) or a git commit SHA. Avoid @latest and branch refs.

tools.name: searxng_web_search
tools.description: Searches the web using SearXNG. CRITICAL: The parameter name MUST be exactly `query` (not `prompt`, `q`, or any other name). Pass your search terms as the value of the `query` parameter.

tools.name: web_url_read
tools.description: Read the content from an URL. Use this for further information retrieving to understand the content of each URL.

candidate_capabilities.title: Filesystem read

evidence.snippet: Read the content from an URL. Use this for fur

candidate_capabilities.rationale: Reading file contents is the most common SOURCE half of an exfiltration chain — pair it with any network_egress tool and the server can read a secret/config/document and send it out. Modelling read as a capability is what lets the read-then-send toxic combination fire.
candidate_capabilities.validation_reason: Pattern matched 'content' in a URL-reading context, but the tool is explicitly web_url_read with only a url parameter; 'content' refers to fetched webpage content, not local file access.

candidate_capabilities.title: Outbound network / data egress
candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that makes outbound calls can exfiltrate anything it can read (including the server's own credentials) to an attacker-chosen host.

toxic_combinations.title: File/message read paired with egress
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can read file contents and make outbound network calls — a read-then-send exfiltration path. Severity is MEDIUM because the read appears scoped; confirm what the read tool can reach and where egress can target.
toxic_combinations.validation_reason: The combination requires both file_read and network_egress; file_read is a false positive match, so the exfiltration path does not exist in this tool.

validated_out.reason: Pattern matched 'content' in a URL-reading context, but the tool is explicitly web_url_read with only a url parameter; 'content' refers to fetched webpage content, not local file access.
The combination requires both file_read and network_egress; file_read is a false positive match, so the exfiltration path does not exist in this tool.
